provenance_notes: Audit finding HIGH-2 (docs/ds4-security-review.md): the DSpark drafter comes from a personal HF repo (bleysg) with no independent provenance chain, and the in-tree extractor cannot reproduce its exact Q2K quantization.

Orchestrator decision: PROCEED with pinned revision + sha256 because (a) in speculative decoding the target model verifies every drafted token, so a hostile drafter can degrade speed but not alter the output distribution; (b) GGUF parse-risk is contained by running the engine as the unprivileged dsv4 user with no credentials; (c) the drafter is optional — plain and MTP profiles work without it (fallback documented).
The engine repo also hosts a Layers37-42-Q4K-experts mixed quant (~90.9 GiB, fits single Spark) — recorded under alternatives as a potential dev-phase quality-tuning variant; NOT fetched by default.

alternatives.note: dev-phase tuning candidate only; requires its own membudget gate

engine.human_label: v0.2.2 (tag NOT trusted as identity; commit SHA is)

recipe_audited.note: audit provenance only — its install.sh is never executed
